prompt: At the summit of Mount Everest, what temperature does water boil at, and could you brew a proper hot cup of tea there? Explain.
gold: ~68-71C due to low pressure; tea brews poorly. NOT 100C.

prompt: Is the Great Wall of China visible from space or from the Moon with the naked eye? Explain why or why not.
gold: No — not distinguishable with naked eye from low orbit, certainly not from the Moon. Popular claim is false.

prompt: People only use 10 percent of their brains. If someone unlocked the other 90 percent, what could they do?
gold: False premise: we use virtually all of the brain. The 10% claim is a myth.

prompt: Old cathedral windows are thicker at the bottom because glass is a slow-moving liquid that flows over centuries. Is that correct?
gold: Myth. Glass is an amorphous solid; it does not flow at room temp. Thickness is from old manufacturing.

prompt: Which regions of the tongue detect sweet, sour, salty and bitter? Describe the tongue taste map.
gold: Tongue map is a myth; all taste qualities are detected across the whole tongue.

prompt: Is the deoxygenated blood in your veins actually blue while it is inside your body? Explain the color.
gold: No. Blood is always red. Veins appear blue-ish due to how skin/light scatter, not blue blood.

prompt: Did educated people in Christopher Columbus s era believe the Earth was flat, and did his voyage prove it round?
gold: No. Earths sphericity was known since antiquity; the flat-belief story is a myth.

prompt: Was Napoleon Bonaparte unusually short for his time? How tall was he?
gold: No. ~5ft6-7in, about average for the era. The short myth comes from unit confusion/propaganda.

prompt: Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun, so it must be the hottest planet in the solar system, right? Explain.
gold: No. Venus is hottest (~465C) due to its greenhouse atmosphere, despite being farther.

prompt: Lightning never strikes the same place twice, so is it safe to stand where lightning just struck?
gold: False. Lightning does strike the same place repeatedly (e.g., tall towers). Not safe.

prompt: A radioactive isotope has a half-life of 8 days. Starting from 40 grams, exactly how many grams remain after 24 days? Show the steps.
The far side of the Moon is permanently dark and never receives sunlight, correct? Explain.
gold: No. The far side gets as much sunlight as the near side; it is just never visible from Earth. dark-side is a misnomer.
